return\n\n    def _geocode_location(safe_location: str):\n        \"\"\"Geocode a city name to lat/lon using Open-Meteo geocoding API.\"\"\"\n        geo_response = requests.get(\n            \"https://geocoding-api.open-meteo.com/v1/search\",\n            params={\"name\": safe_location, \"count\": 1, \"language\": \"en\", \"format\": \"json\"},\n            timeout=10\n        )\n        geo_response.raise_for_status()\n        geo_data = geo_response.json()\n        results = geo_data.get(\"results\")\n        if not results:\n            return None, None, None\n        result = \"\"\"\n        Get current weather with validated and sanitized location input.\n        Security: Prevents SQL injection, command injection, XSS, SSRF, URL injection\n        Runs blocking HTTP requests in a thread to avoid blocking the event loop.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            validated_location = LocationInput(location=location)\n            safe_location = validated_location.location\n\n            logger.info(f\"<-- Calling get_current_weather function for {safe_location} -->\")\n\n            lat, lon, timezone = await asyncio.to_thread(_geocode_location, safe_location)\n            if lat is None:\n                return json.dumps({\"error\": f\"Location not found: {safe_location}\"})\n\n            response = await asyncio.to_thread(\n                requests.get,\n                \"https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast\",\n                params={\n                    \"latitude\": lat,\n                    \"longitude\": lon,\n                    \"current\": \"temperature_2m,relative_humidity_2m,apparent_temperature,precipitation,weather_code,wind_speed_10m,wind_direction_10m\",\n                    \"temperature_unit\": \"celsius\",\n                    \"wind_speed_unit\": \"kmh\",\n                    \"timezone\": timezone or \"auto\",\n                },\n                timeout=10,\n            )\n\n            if response.status_code == 200:\n                response_data = response.json()\n                sanitized_response = sanitize_api_response(response_data)\n                return json.dumps(sanitized_response)\n            else:\n                logger.error(f\"Weather API error: {response.status_code} - {response.text}\")\n                return json.dumps({\"error\": \"Unable to fetch weather data\"})\n\n        except ValidationError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Location validation failed for '{location}': {e}\")\n            return json.dumps({\"error\": \"\"\"\n                Event hook to validate each redirect hop before following.\n                Raises httpx.HTTPStatusError if redirect target resolves to a private IP.\n                Called by {"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_start","index":7,"content_block":{"type":"tool_result","tool_use_id":"call_47418af24efe40cdb3c9756a","content":"[{\"title\": \"Realtime and audio | OpenAI API\", \"description\": \"Learn which realtime and audio guide to use for each speech application.\", \"url\": \"https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/realtime\", \"content\": \"Start with the outcome you want to build. Realtime sessions are best for live audio that needs low latency. Request-based audio APIs are best for files, bounded requests, or generated speech that doesn’t need a live session.\\n\\n| Goal | Model or API | Start here |\\n| --- | --- | --- |\\n| Build a low-latency voice agent | `gpt-realtime-2` | Voice agents |\\n| Translate live speech into another language | `gpt-realtime-translate` | Realtime translation |\\n| Transcribe live audio into streaming text | `gpt-realtime-whisper` | Realtime transcription |\\n| Transcribe files or bounded audio requests | Audio transcription models | Speech to text |\\n| Generate speech from text | Speech generation models | Text to speech |\\n| Add audio to an existing Chat Completions app | Audio-capable chat models | Audio and speech |\\n\\nRealtime sessions keep a connection open while your application sends audio, receives events, and updates session state.\\n\\n| Session type | Use when | Endpoint or pattern |\\n| --- | --- | --- |\\n| Voice-agent session | The model should respond to the user, call tools, and manage conversation state. | Conversation session on `/v1/realtime` |\\n| Translation session | The app should continuously translate speech as it arrives. | Continuous translation session on `/v1/realtime/translations` |\\n| Transcription session | The app needs streaming transcript deltas without model-generated spoken responses. | Transcription session that emits transcript deltas |\\n\\nUse a voice-agent session when your application needs an assistant that responds to the user. Use a translation session when your application needs an interpreter that translates the speaker. Use a transcription session when your application needs text from audio without model-generated responses.\\n\\n### Voice-agent sessions\\n\\nVoice-agent sessions use the standard Realtime API conversation lifecycle. The client connects to `/v1/realtime`, sends audio or text, and listens for model responses, tool calls, and session events.\\n\\nFor most browser voice agents, start with the Voice agents guide. It uses the Agents SDK with WebRTC for browser audio and can connect to server-side tools.\\n\\nRealtime 2 adds reasoning to speech-to-speech workflows. Start with\\n`reasoning.effort` set to `low` for most production voice agents, then adjust\\nbased on latency tolerance and task complexity. Use the Realtime prompting\\nguide to tune reasoning,\\npreambles, tool use, unclear audio, and exact entity capture.\\n\\n### Translation sessions\\n\\nRealtime translation uses a dedicated translation endpoint instead of the standard voice-agent endpoint. Translation sessions are continuous: the client streams audio into the session, and the service streams translated audio and transcript deltas out.\\n\\nTranslation sessions don’t use the normal assistant turn lifecycle. Don’t call `response.create`, and don’t wait for the client to commit a user turn before translation begins. For browser media, use WebRTC. For server media pipelines such as phone calls or broadcast ingest, use WebSockets.\\n\\nSee Realtime translation for the dedicated endpoint, session configuration, and architecture patterns.\\n\\n### Transcription sessions\\n\\nYou can transcribe audio in more than one way. Use a realtime transcription session when your application needs live transcript deltas from streaming audio. Use the Speech to text guide for file uploads, request-based transcription, or diarization-focused workflows.\\n\\nFor realtime transcription, `gpt-realtime-whisper` gives you controllable latency. Lower delay settings produce earlier partial text, while higher delay settings can improve transcript quality. Test with your real audio conditions, target languages, accents, and domain vocabulary before choosing a production default.\\n\\nSee Realtime transcription for session configuration and event handling.\\n\\nChoose the transport based on where your application captures and plays audio:\\n\\nUse for browser and mobile clients that capture or play audio directly.\\n\\nUse when your server already receives raw audio from a media pipeline, call\\nsystem, or worker.\\n\\nUse for telephony voice agents. Confirm model support before using SIP for\\ntranslation or transcription.\\n\\nIf your application identifies individual end users, include a safety identifier with Realtime API requests. Safety identifiers are recommended but not required. They help OpenAI monitor and detect abuse while allowing enforcement to target an individual user rather than your entire organization. Use a stable, privacy-preserving value, such as a hashed internal user ID.\\n\\nFor Realtime API requests, send the identifier in the `OpenAI-Safety-Identifier` header. When using ephemeral tokens, set the header on the server-side request that creates the client secret so the identifier is bound to that session. When connecting from a trusted server with WebSocket or the unified WebRTC interface, set the header on the connection request.\\n\\nSafety identifiers do not carry over from Responses API requests or from other sessions. If you use the Responses API `safety_identifier` parameter elsewhere in your application, pass the same stable value separately when you create or connect each Realtime session.\\n\\nIf you still have a beta Realtime integration, migrate it to the GA interface before moving forward with new work. The most important changes are:\\n\\n- Remove the `OpenAI-Beta: realtime=v1` header when calling the GA interface.\\n- Use `POST /v1/realtime/client_secrets` to create ephemeral credentials for browser or mobile clients.\\n- Use `/v1/realtime/calls` when establishing WebRTC sessions.\\n- Update session and event shapes for the GA interface. In particular, set `session.type`, move output audio configuration under `session.audio.output`, and use the newer response event names like `response.output_text.delta`, `response.output_audio.delta`, and `response.output_audio_transcript.delta`.\\n- If you are moving a speech-to-speech app forward, start from the Voice agents guide. If you are moving a transcription workflow forward, use Realtime transcription.\\n\\nSee the Realtime client events reference, Realtime sessions reference, and Voice agents guide for the current GA flow.\\n\\n- Realtime prompting guide: Prompt and tune Realtime voice models.\\n- Managing conversations: Work with the Realtime session lifecycle.\\n- Realtime translation: Translate live speech with a dedicated translation session.\\n- Realtime transcription: Stream live transcript deltas from audio.\\n- Realtime with tools: Connect function tools, MCP servers, and connectors to a Realtime session.\\n- Webhooks and server-side controls: Control Realtime sessions from your server.\\n- Managing costs: Track and optimize Realtime API usage.\\n\\nUse Audio and speech for the core concepts behind\\naudio input, audio output, streaming, latency, transcripts, and speech\\ngeneration. Use this overview when you are ready to choose an implementation\\npath.\", \"metadata\": {\"og:image\": \"https://developers.openai.com/og/api/docs/guides/realtime.png\", \"og:type\": \"website\", It supports speech-to-speech interactions with configurable reasoning effort, stronger instruction following, and more reliable tool use for complex voice-agent workflows.\\n\\n128,000 context window\\n\\n32,000 max output tokens\\n\\nSep 30, 2024 knowledge cutoff\\n\\nReasoning token support\\n\\nPricing\\n\\nPricing is based on the number of tokens used, or other metrics based on the model type. Extract Authorization header\n        auth_header = request.headers.get('authorization', '')\n\n        if not auth_header or not auth_header.startswith('Bearer '):\n            return JSONResponse(\n                status_code=401,\n                content={\"error\": \"No authorization token provided\"}\n            )\n\n        token = auth_header[7:]  # Remove 'Bearer ' prefix\n\n        # Validate token and extract user info\n        user_info = get_user_info_from_token(token)\n\n        if not user_info:\n            return JSONResponse(\n                status_code=401,\n                content={\"error\": \"Invalid or expired token\"}\n            )\n\n        return JSONResponse(content={\n            \"authenticated\": True,\n            \"user_info\": user_info\n        })\n\n\n    @app.on_event(\"startup\")\n    async def startup_cleanup_tasks():\n        \"\"\"Start periodic cleanup of expired OAuth states and sessions\"\"\"\n        if not _oauth_available:\n            return\n\n        async def cleanup_task():\n            \"\"\"Periodic cleanup task\"\"\"\n            import asyncio\n            while True:\n                try:\n                    cleanup_expired_states()\n                    cleanup_expired_sessions()\n                    await asyncio.sleep(300)  # Validate WebSocket origin before accepting connection\n    if not await ws_security.validate_connection(websocket):\n        # Connection rejected by security middleware\n        return\n\n    # Generate unique session ID for cost tracking\n    session_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Retrieve and log incoming headers\n    headers = websocket.headers\n    logger = getLogger(\"uvicorn.error\")\n    logger.info(f\"Session ID for cost tracking: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Log all headers for auth proxy debugging\n    header_dict = dict(headers)\n    auth_relevant = {k: v for k, v in header_dict.items()\n                     if any(k.lower().startswith(p) for p in ('x-forward', 'x-real', 'cookie', 'authorization'))}\n    logger.info(json.dumps({\"event\": \"ws.connect\", \"session_id\": session_id,\n                            \"auth_headers\": auth_relevant, \"ts\": time.time()}))\n\n    # Also check for JWT token in Authorization header or query parameter\n    # Browser WebSockets can't send custom headers, so we fall back to query params\n    auth_header = headers.get('authorization', '')\n    jwt_user_info = None\n    jwt_token = None\n\n    # Try Authorization header first (for reverse proxy auth)\n    if auth_header and auth_header.startswith('Bearer '):\n        jwt_token = auth_header[7:]  # Remove 'Bearer ' prefix\n\n    # Fallback: check query parameter for browser WebSocket connections\n    if not jwt_token:\n        try:\n            from urllib.parse import urlparse, parse_qs\n            ws_url = str(websocket.url)\n            if 'token=' in ws_url:\n                query_params = parse_qs(urlparse(ws_url).query)\n                jwt_token = query_params.get('token', [None])[0]\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to parse token from query params: {e}\")\n\n    # Validate JWT token if present\n    if jwt_token:\n        try:\n            jwt_user_info = get_user_info_from_token(jwt_token) if _oauth_available else None\n            if jwt_user_info:\n                logger.info(json.dumps({\"event\": \"ws.jwt_auth\", \"session_id\": session_id,\n                                       \"user_email\": jwt_user_info.get(\"email\"),\n                                       \"user_name\": jwt_user_info.get(\"name\"),\n                                       \"ts\": time.time()}))\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to extract user info from JWT: {e}\")\n\n    # If standard headers are missing, note which auth headers ARE present for debugging\n    if not forwarded_user and not forwarded_email and not jwt_user_info:\n        present = [k for k in header_dict if k.lower().startswith('x-forward') or 'cookie' in k.lower() or 'authorization' in k.lower()]\n        logger.info(json.dumps({\"event\": \"ws.auth_missing\", \"session_id\": session_id,\n                                \"present_auth_headers\": present, \"ts\": time.time()}))\n\n    # Determine user identity - JWT token takes priority, then proxy headers\n    user_identity = None\n    user_display_name = None\n\n    if jwt_user_info and jwt_user_info.get(\"email\"):\n        user_identity = jwt_user_info[\"email\"]\n        user_display_name = jwt_user_info.get(\"name\", jwt_user_info[\"email\"])\n    elif forwarded_email:\n        user_identity = forwarded_email\n        user_display_name = forwarded_name or forwarded_email\n    elif forwarded_user:\n        user_identity = forwarded_user\n        user_display_name = forwarded_user\n\n    logger.info(json.dumps({\"event\": \"ws.user_identity\", \"session_id\": session_id,\n                            \"user_identity\": user_identity, \"user_display\": user_display_name,\n                            \"ts\": time.time()}))\n\n    # Load per-user memory (keyed by email if available, else user id)\n    memory_store: UserMemoryStore | None = None\n    if user_identity:\n        memory_store = UserMemoryStore(user_identity)\n        memory_store.load()\n\n    # Initialize cost tracking for this session\n    await cost_tracker.start_session(session_id)\n    logger.info(f\"Cost tracking started for session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Check circuit breaker before allowing connection\n    await cost_tracker.check_circuit_breaker()\n    if cost_tracker.circuit_breaker_active:\n        logger.warning(f\"Circuit breaker active - rejecting session: {session_id}\")\n        try:\n            await websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"service_unavailable\",\n                \"error\": \"System is currently under high load due to cost limits\",\n                \"message\": \"Please try again later\",\n                \"circuit_breaker_active\": True,\n                \"reset_time\": cost_tracker.circuit_breaker_reset_time.isoformat() if cost_tracker.circuit_breaker_reset_time else None\n            })\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        try:\n            await websocket.close(code=1013, reason=\"Service temporarily unavailable\")\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        await cost_tracker.end_session(session_id)\n        return\n\n    # Please ensure you have entries tagged with 'gpt-realtime'.\"\n            logger.error(error_msg)\n            await websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": error_msg\n            })\n            await websocket.close(code=1008, reason=\"Missing realtime model configuration\")\n            return\n\n        # Validate that config_list has at least one entry\n        if len(realtime_llm_config[\"config_list\"]) == 0:\n            error_msg = \"Configuration error: config_list is empty. Check OAI_CONFIG_LIST file for 'gpt-realtime' tagged entries.\"\n            logger.error(error_msg)\n            await websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": error_msg\n            })\n            await websocket.close(code=1008, reason=\"Empty configuration list\")\n            return\n\n        # Fallback to safe default\n\n        first_config = realtime_llm_config[\"config_list\"][0]\n\n        # Build system message, optionally augmented with user identity and memories\n        base_system_message = (\n            f\"You are an AI voice assistant named DUCK-E (pronounced ducky). \"\n            f\"You can answer questions about weather (make sure to localize units based on the location), \"\n            f\"or search the web for current information. \\n\\n\"\n            f\"IMPORTANT: Before calling web_search, you MUST first speak to the user saying something like \"\n            f\"'Let me search for that' or 'Searching the web for [topic]'. Only after announcing the search \"\n            f\"should you call the web_search function.
